description_long: This is a heavy-duty pipe wrench with a robust steel body and a bright red painted handle. The handle is elongated and features a black grip section, likely made of a different material for improved ergonomics. The wrench head is metallic gray, with an adjustable upper jaw and a knurled adjustment nut for changing the jaw opening. The jaws are serrated, designed to grip round objects such as pipes securely. The tool's construction suggests durability and suitability for plumbing or mechanical work in urban environments. The overall finish is clean and industrial, with no visible signs of rust or wear.
description: A sturdy, red and black pipe wrench with a metallic adjustable jaw and a textured handle grip. The tool is designed for gripping and turning pipes, featuring a robust steel build and a knurled adjustment nut.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the narrow profile of the wrench, with the adjustment nut and jaw barely visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the wrench, the red handle, black grip, and the metallic adjustable jaw with the adjustment nut.
description_view_2: Handle-end view highlighting the black grip and the slimness of the handle, with the jaw and adjustment nut seen from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the red handle, black grip, and metallic jaw, but from the other side.
attribute_handle_grip_pattern: The handle grip area is black with visible longitudinal grooves, providing a textured, non-slip surface.
